name: Rocket Lab Corporation

profile.description: Rocket Lab is an end-to-end space company providing launch services (Electron small-lift, Neutron medium-lift in development) and space systems (spacecraft components, satellite manufacturing). Q1 2026 delivered record $200.3M revenue (64% YoY growth), $2.2B backlog, and 31 new launch contracts. The company is the second most frequently launched US orbital rocket after SpaceX.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): Rocket Lab grew Q1 2026 revenue 64% to a record $200.3M, with space systems ($136.7M) now larger than launch ($63.7M), representing a successful business model evolution from pure launch to full-stack space company [1]. Electron achieved 100% success rate across 21 launches in 2025, and the company pioneered 3D-printed rocket engines and electric-pump-fed propulsion [2]. However, Neutron — the rocket the valuation depends on — has still not flown. AI Deployment (0/2): No evidence of AI-powered products shipping or AI-driven cost savings being reported. Rocket Lab is a physical engineering company with no material AI deployment [2]. Market Share (2/2): Electron is the second most frequently launched US orbital rocket after SpaceX Falcon 9, with dominant share in the dedicated small-launch market globally. The $816M SDA prime satellite contract validates Rocket Lab as a credible competitor to established defense primes [1]. Pipeline (2/2): Neutron first flight targeted later 2026 with five contracts already signed; $2.2B backlog more than doubled YoY; Q2 guidance of $225-240M exceeded analyst expectations of $205M [1]. R&D Intensity (1/1): Massive R&D investment in Neutron development, Archimedes engine qualification, and spacecraft subsystems relative to the company's $800M+ revenue base.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): Founder-CEO Peter Beck's stated purpose — 'We go to space to improve life on Earth' — is specific, service-oriented, and lived out through execution. Beck built the company from scratch in New Zealand with the conviction that a team built on audacity and discipline could reinvent an industry [2]. The mission guides product decisions and has been consistent since founding. However, the company is too young (~10 years public) to earn the maximum score for decades-validated execution. Community Impact (1/2): Rocket Lab employs 2,600 specialized aerospace engineers across the US and New Zealand, contributing to sovereign space capability. The SDA satellite program supports national defense infrastructure [1]. Employee Retention (1/2): Achieving 100% launch success across 21 missions requires extraordinary team cohesion. However, Beck openly states 'if you want work-life balance, you shouldn't join' and competitors don't have it either, suggesting a demanding culture that may not retain all talent [2]. Insider Buying (0/2): No evidence of meaningful net insider buying in the last 12 months. Beck retains significant founder equity but no recent open-market purchases found.

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Rocket Lab's infrastructure reflects a high-growth pre-profit space company investing heavily for the future. Revenue growth is exceptional at 63.5% YoY with Q1 2026 hitting $200.3M and Q2 guidance of $225-240M [1]. Gross margin improved to 38.2% from the low 30s a year ago, and net loss narrowed to $45M from $60.6M [1]. D/E of just 6.1% is conservative with $1.38B cash against $139M debt. However, profit margin remains deeply negative at -26.9%, ROE is -13.5%, and forward P/E of 18,247x reflects near-zero earnings expectations. Free cash flow is negative $215M. The stock at $105.47 with a $61B market cap prices in enormous future success including Neutron's unproven revenue contribution. Liquidity floor easily cleared: 22.1M avg volume × $105.47 = $2.33B daily.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): Rocket science, launch operations, and satellite manufacturing are physical-world operations involving propulsion engineering, materials science, orbital mechanics, and precision manufacturing that AI structurally cannot replicate or substitute [1]. Rocket Lab's core competencies require hardware-in-the-loop engineering that has no AI-based alternative. Moreover, AI data centers and satellite communications infrastructure increase demand for the space systems and launches Rocket Lab provides, making the company an indirect AI beneficiary. AI Opportunity (0/1): Rocket Lab does not generate direct AI revenue, though its satellites support AI-enabled defense surveillance networks and its Photon spacecraft bus could serve AI-driven Earth observation missions [1].

infinite_game.summary: Rocket Lab under founder-CEO Peter Beck demonstrates strong infinite-game characteristics: building Electron over a decade before achieving scale, developing Neutron as a long-term medium-lift platform, and accepting persistent losses to build a full-stack space company. Beck's 'if you're afraid to fail, you'll never innovate' philosophy and the company's expansion from launch to satellite manufacturing show genuine long-term thinking. The $2.2B backlog and 100% launch success validate patient execution.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): 'We go to space to improve life on Earth' is a genuine, inclusive, and service-oriented cause that Peter Beck has lived out since founding the company [2]. The mission is bigger than any single product — it guided the expansion from launch to space systems to satellite manufacturing. Beck built the company with the conviction that 'audacity tempered by discipline' could reinvent the space industry. However, at ~10 years, the company hasn't yet validated this cause through the multi-decade crisis-tested consistency that earns the maximum score.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): Achieving 100% launch success across 21 missions in 2025 requires exceptional team discipline and coordination — this is a high-trust operational achievement [1]. The 2,600-person team simultaneously delivered record revenue while developing Neutron, SDA satellites, and spacecraft subsystems. However, Beck openly acknowledges the culture demands extreme work intensity with 'no work-life balance,' which while honest may create burnout risk and limit the Circle of Safety [2].

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): Rocket Lab explicitly positions SpaceX as a worthy rival whose dominance drives Rocket Lab to innovate rather than a competitor to destroy [2]. Neutron is designed to compete in SpaceX's Falcon 9 market segment, but Rocket Lab differentiates through vertical integration and specialized mission services rather than trying to out-SpaceX SpaceX. Beck's competitive framing is consistently about self-improvement.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): Rocket Lab has expanded meaningfully from launch-only to a diversified space infrastructure provider — space systems revenue ($136.7M in Q1) now exceeds launch revenue ($63.7M), representing a genuine business model evolution [1]. The $816M SDA prime satellite contract puts Rocket Lab in the satellite manufacturing business competing against established defense primes. However, the company is young enough that this represents natural growth-phase expansion rather than a painful, transformative pivot from a working model.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (2/2): Building Neutron — a reusable medium-lift rocket — from scratch while absorbing years of development costs and persistent operating losses demonstrates genuine long-term conviction [1]. The decision to pursue the SDA prime contract and compete against Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman, and other established defense primes as a 2,600-person company requires extraordinary strategic courage. Beck's willingness to delay Neutron for engineering integrity over rushing to market shows principled leadership [2].

investment_thesis: Rocket Lab delivered record Q1 2026 revenue of $200.3M (64% YoY growth), with backlog more than doubling to $2.2B and gross margin expanding to 38.2% [1]. The company signed 31 new Electron/HASTE contracts plus 5 Neutron contracts in the quarter, and Q2 guidance of $225-240M exceeded analyst expectations by 15%+. Space systems revenue ($136.7M) now dominates launch ($63.7M), validating the transition to a full-stack space company. With $1.38B cash, minimal debt (6% D/E), and the Electron's proven 100% success rate, Rocket Lab has operational credibility that de-risks the Neutron development timeline targeting first flight later 2026 [1].
thesis_continued: The bear case is valuation and execution risk. At $105.47 with a $61B market cap, Rocket Lab trades at 18,247x forward earnings on deeply negative profitability (net loss of $45M in Q1, profit margin of -27%) [1]. The entire investment case rests on Neutron's successful development and market capture — a rocket that has still not flown. If Neutron experiences further delays, cost overruns, or competitive pressure from SpaceX Starship and emerging launchers, the premium valuation has no floor. Free cash flow remains negative at -$215M annually, requiring continued capital markets access that dilution risk accompanies. The stock has surged from $67 to $105 in weeks following Q1 results, pricing in near-perfect execution on a program with significant remaining technical risk [1]. Beck's demanding no-work-life-balance culture may challenge talent retention as the team scales for Neutron production [2].
key_catalysts: Neutron medium-lift rocket first flight targeted later 2026, with 5 contracts already signed and Archimedes engine qualification progressing — a successful debut would validate the $61B valuation [1]
Q2 2026 revenue guidance of $225-240M representing continued 50%+ growth, with $2.2B backlog providing multi-year visibility [1]
Space systems becoming the dominant revenue segment validates Rocket Lab's evolution from launch provider to full-stack space infrastructure company [1]
key_risks: Extreme valuation at $61B market cap with negative profitability — Neutron must succeed for the investment case to hold, and any execution miss could trigger severe multiple compression
Neutron has not yet flown despite the valuation depending on it — first flight delays, manufacturing defects, or competitive pressure from SpaceX Starship create binary outcome risk [1]
Persistent negative free cash flow (-$215M) and negative profit margin (-27%) require continued capital markets access, creating dilution risk at elevated share prices [1]
sources: Rocket Lab Q1 2026 Earnings Release — $200.3M record revenue, 64% growth, $2.2B backlog, 31 new contracts, May 8, 2026
Quartr, Bessemer Venture Partners, BusinessDesk NZ — Peter Beck leadership philosophy, Rocket Lab culture and innovation approach, 2025-2026
Yahoo Finance, Sustainalytics — RKLB key statistics, ESG ratings and environmental transparency, 2025-2026
